auth_notes: Most industry leaders (Samsara, Motive, Geotab) utilize Bearer Token authentication. Some require an additional 'X-API-Key' header or a specific 'Fleet ID' parameter in the request URL.
rate_limits: Typically 10 requests per second for standard tiers; Geotab and Motive use daily quotas ranging from 5,000 to 50,000 calls.
gotchas: High-frequency GPS pings can quickly exhaust rate limits if polling is used instead of webhooks.
Pagination is usually cursor-based, requiring recursive workflow loops in n8n.
Timezone handling for HOS (Hours of Service) logs is often inconsistent between different ELD providers.
client_must_provide: List of specific Asset/Vehicle IDs to monitor
operations_available: Retrieve Driver Hours of Service (HOS) Logs
research_notes: Since no native node exists, the HTTP Request node is mandatory. Users should implement a 'Split In Batches' node when fetching fleet-wide data to prevent memory issues. For real-time tracking, the Webhook node should be used to receive 'Geofence' or 'Status Change' triggers directly from the ELD provider.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Since no native node exists, the HTTP Request node is mandatory. Users should implement a 'Split In Batches' node when fetching fleet-wide data to prevent memory issues. For real-time tracking, the Webhook node should be used to receive 'Geofence' or 'Status Change' triggers directly from the ELD provider.
